title: 1981-04-16_Pat_103.txt
key_claims: The Tagore vision finally answers Dick's lifelong trauma regarding unmerited suffering (the burning Japanese soldier, napalm victims, his sister Jane's fatal burns, abused animals). The answer is that it is *always* Christ who suffers in them, and He does so voluntarily.

To maintain his sanity, Dick replaces the inscrutable mystery of unmerited suffering with the inscrutable mystery of God's "absolute beauty." He accepts this beauty as the irreducible core of reality.
He realizes that the explosion of mystical consciousness in 2-3-74 was essentially his entire life "played at high-speed fast forward," triggered by the perception of his own imminent death.
The 9-81 Tagore vision completes the cycle that 1974 started, answering both the mysteries of his mystical experiences and the existential questions of his entire human life.
recurring_concepts: **The Invading Rational**: The Logos (Christ) is structure triumphing over chaos; it sobers the landscape and cures the madness and suffering of the irrational universe.
reading_excerpt: The Tagore vision finally answers Dick's lifelong trauma regarding unmerited suffering (the burning Japanese soldier, napalm victims, his sister Jane's fatal burns, abused animals). The answer is that it is *always* Christ who suffers in them, and He does so voluntarily.
